text.title: Project Structure and JPA Entities
text.summary: Spring Boot 3.x layered project layout, BaseEntity with auditing, and User entity with JPA annotations.

rule.statement: Follow the layered package structure: controller/, service/, service/impl/, repository/, entity/, dto/request/, dto/response/, mapper/, exception/. Never place business logic in controllers or entities as data-only bags.
rule.rationale: Spring Boot's layered architecture enforces separation of concerns. The structure matches the AOP proxy model: @Transactional works correctly only when calls cross Spring bean boundaries.

example.code: src/main/java/com/example/app/ ├── controller/ │ ├── UserController.java │ └── advice/GlobalExceptionHandler.java ├── service/ │ ├── UserService.java (interface) │ └── impl/UserServiceImpl.java ├── repository/ │ ├── UserRepository.java │ └── specification/UserSpecification.java ├── entity/ │ ├── BaseEntity.java │ └── User.java ├── dto/ │ ├── request/CreateUserRequest.java │ └── response/UserResponse.java ├── mapper/UserMapper.java └── exception/ ├── ResourceNotFoundException.java └── BusinessException.java

rule.statement: All persistent entities extend BaseEntity which provides UUID primary key, createdAt, updatedAt via @CreationTimestamp/@UpdateTimestamp, and optimistic locking via @Version.
rule.rationale: Centralising audit fields in a superclass avoids repetition. @Version provides optimistic locking without explicit code; concurrent updates fail fast with OptimisticLockException instead of silently overwriting.

rule.statement: Annotate the Spring Boot application class with @EnableJpaAuditing. Without it, @CreationTimestamp and @UpdateTimestamp do not fire and audit fields remain null.
rule.rationale: JPA auditing is opt-in in Spring Boot 3. Missing @EnableJpaAuditing is a frequent agent error that only surfaces in integration tests when audit fields are asserted.

rule.statement: Entities must contain at least one domain method per non-trivial behaviour (e.g., promote(), addPost(), deactivate()). Service classes should orchestrate, not hold every business rule.
rule.rationale: Purely anemic entities (only getters/setters) push all logic into services, making it impossible to enforce invariants at the entity level. Entity methods can throw IllegalStateException on invalid transitions without a service call.

antipattern.description: @Data on JPA entities — Lombok generates equals/hashCode using all fields.
antipattern.reason: Entity equality must be based on identity (ID), not value. @Data-generated equals causes incorrect Set behaviour for managed entities and can produce infinite loops with bidirectional relationships.
